diagrams.title: Traditional vs Agile Project Management Comparison
diagrams.description: A comparative view showing differences between traditional (Waterfall) and modern (Agile) approaches across multiple dimensions
elements: Traditional: Sequential phases vs Agile: Iterative sprints
Traditional: Fixed requirements vs Agile: Evolving requirements
Traditional: Limited customer involvement vs Agile: Continuous collaboration
Traditional: Heavy documentation vs Agile: Working software focus
Traditional: Late testing vs Agile: Continuous testing
Traditional: Single delivery vs Agile: Incremental delivery
diagrams.purpose: Understand key differences to choose appropriate methodology

diagrams.title: Scrum Framework Overview
diagrams.description: The complete Scrum process flow showing roles, events, and artifacts
elements: Development Team delivers potentially shippable Increment
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete Scrum workflow and understand ceremonies and artifacts

diagrams.title: Kanban Board Layout
diagrams.description: A typical Kanban board showing workflow visualization with WIP limits
elements: In Progress column (Active work with WIP limit)
diagrams.purpose: Understand visual workflow management and WIP limiting concepts

diagrams.title: Agile Manifesto Values
diagrams.description: The four core values of Agile Manifesto with their contrasts
elements: Individuals and interactions > processes and tools
Responding to change > following a plan
diagrams.purpose: Memorize and understand the foundational values of Agile methodology

visuals.title: Traditional Versus Modern Project Management Practices
visuals.description: Visual diagram for traditional versus modern project management practices
